data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Harrow Health, Citing Expanding Ophthalmic Franchise and Long-Term Growth Pipeline
data.description:
William Blair analyst Lachlan Hanbury Brown has maintained their bullish stance on HROW stock, giving a Buy rating today.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Lachlan Hanbury Brown has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Harrow Health’s expanding ophthalmic franchise and recent execution milestones. The commercial introduction of Byooviz, through an exclusive U.S. partnership with Samsung Bioepis, broadens Harrow’s presence in the large anti-VEGF retina market and adds a new, albeit initially modest, revenue contributor.

Beyond Byooviz, Brown highlights Harrow’s growing portfolio of FDA-approved products, including Vevye, Iheezo, and Triesence, which together reinforce the company’s positioning in U.S. ophthalmic pharmaceuticals. While acknowledging that periodic demand patterns may cause share-price fluctuations, he views the company’s execution and product pipeline—such as future commercialization of Opuviz—as underpinning a compelling long-term growth profile that supports a Buy recommendation.

Hanbury Brown covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Harrow Health, Viridian Therapeutics, and Ocular Therapeutix. According to TipRanks, Hanbury Brown has an average return of 1.3% and a 41.30% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Lake Street also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $60.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Levi Strauss, Sees Earnings Upside and Margin Expansion with Unchanged $27 Price Target
data.description:
Levi Strauss & Co, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Kendall Toscano from Bank of America Securities maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $27.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Kendall Toscano has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both earnings and valuation upside for Levi Strauss & Co. He views the current ~15x 2027 P/E as attractive for a global brand still early in its shift toward more durable, higher-quality growth, with near-term results supported by healthy underlying demand and strong direct-to-consumer performance despite a temporary wholesale timing headwind in Europe.

He also emphasizes margin expansion potential, noting benefits from a more favorable tariff environment, disciplined pricing, and cost efficiencies that should increasingly support EBIT margins in the second half and over the long term. With sustained mid-single-digit or better sales growth, improving flow-through, and upside from tariff savings and possible refunds, Toscano believes consensus earnings estimates can move higher, justifying his unchanged $27 price target and continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released on June 26, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $34.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Frasers Group: Buy Rating as Sports Direct Malaysia Sale to MAP Active Unlocks Cash, Boosts Asset-Light Model Ahead of FY26 Results
data.description:
Frasers Group, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Andrew Wade from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a p1,100.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Andrew Wade has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably the strategic sale of Frasers’ stake in Sports Direct Malaysia to MAP Active for US$150m. He views this transaction as a value-accretive move that monetizes the asset while securing a long-term income stream, as MAP Active assumes responsibility for expanding the Sports Direct brand in Malaysia using its established local capabilities.

This deal also aligns Malaysia with the asset-light structure that Frasers already uses in other MAP-linked markets, improving capital efficiency across the partnership. In Wade’s assessment, the transaction generates over £100m of cash for Frasers, strengthens the balance sheet, and supports future growth or capital allocation, all of which underpin his positive stance and Buy recommendation on the stock ahead of the upcoming FY26 results.

According to TipRanks, Wade is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 3.2% and a 51.43% success rate. Wade covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Games Workshop, Frasers Group, and AO World.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Sell Rating and Unchanged $152 Price Target as Valuation Skews Risk/Reward to Downside
data.description:
Constellation Brands, the Consumer Defensive sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Peter Galbo from Bank of America Securities maintained a Sell rating on the stock and has a $152.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Peter Galbo has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors that temper the otherwise solid quarterly results. While first-quarter earnings and beer sales exceeded expectations, the upside was largely driven by shipment timing, tax, and interest benefits rather than a clear acceleration in underlying demand, with beer depletions slightly negative and retail consumption trends pointing to modest volume declines.

At the same time, management merely reaffirmed its fiscal 2027 outlook, signaling limited incremental growth momentum in a still-uncertain consumer and category environment. Galbo also notes that margins and free cash flow are broadly in line with prior guidance, and with the stock already discounting much of the long-term plan at roughly 12.5x projected 2027 earnings, he views the risk/reward as skewed to the downside, justifying an Underperform rating and an unchanged $152 price target.

According to TipRanks, Galbo is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 2.1% and a 54.21% success rate. Galbo covers the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on stocks such as Coca-Cola, Constellation Brands, and Primo Brands.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on Schlumberger as Analyst Sees Long-Term Growth Upside and Keeps $63 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Schlumberger, the Energy sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Phillip Jungwirth from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $63.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Phillip Jungwirth has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Schlumberger’s resilient near-term outlook and attractive long-term growth profile despite temporary Middle East disruptions. While he modestly reduced 2Q and 3Q estimates to reflect regional activity delays, he still expects a meaningful acceleration in 4Q26 and through 2027 as Middle East operations normalize and deepwater projects ramp, supporting stronger revenues, margins, and earnings.

He highlights SLB’s leading position in international oilfield services, robust EBITDA and free cash flow margins, and strong technology offering, particularly in Digital and subsea, as key competitive advantages. Jungwirth also points to catalysts such as realizing the full value of the Digital segment, integrating ChampionX, and a recovery in Saudi and offshore activity, which underpin his unchanged $63 price target and support a positive risk-reward skew for the stock.

Jungwirth covers the Energy sector, focusing on stocks such as Schlumberger, Talos Energy, and Chevron. According to TipRanks, Jungwirth has an average return of 6.2% and a 49.12% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, Bernstein also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $71.00 price target.
